articles.title: Digital pathology-based artificial intelligence models for differential diagnosis and prognosis of sporadic odontogenic keratocysts
articles.authors: Cai X; Zhang H; Wang Y; et al.
articles.keywords: odontogenic keratocyst; digital pathology; artificial intelligence; Inception_v3; prognosis
articles.abstract: 519 OKC cases comprising 2,157 H&E-stained images were used to develop digital pathology-based AI models for OKC diagnosis and prognosis. Inception_v3 was trained on patch-level images. Whole slide image-level AI models were developed by integrating deep learning pathology features with machine learning algorithms. Models distinguished OKC from orthokeratinized odontogenic cyst and other jaw cysts and predicted recurrence risk. The AI model shows strong potential for clinical application in odontogenic jaw diseases.

articles.title: CLASEG: advanced multiclassification and segmentation for differential diagnosis of oral lesions using deep learning
articles.keywords: oral lesions; deep learning; EfficientNet-B3; Mask R-CNN; classification; segmentation; oral cancer
articles.abstract: A novel deep learning framework for multi-class classification-based segmentation enabling accurate differential diagnosis of 14 common oral lesions across various mouth locations using photographic images. A dataset of 2,072 clinical images was used. The framework integrates EfficientNet-B3 for classification and ResNet-101-based Mask R-CNN for segmentation, achieving classification accuracy of 74.49% and segmentation AP50 of 72.18. Grad-CAM was applied for visualization of influential areas for predictive decisions.
